English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
George G. Heye autograph collection
Heye, George G. (George Gustav), 1874-1957.
Huntington Free Library
9063
1 folders. 1 folders.
1886-1928. Correspondence Collection of autographs of individuals associated with George Gustav Heye, Frederick W. Hodge, and Indians of North America. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 - folder 2 ======= 1 - folder 2 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 5678793
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Evan Fay Earle
Date completed:
February 2006
EAD encoding:
Evan Fay Earle February 2006
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection of autographs of individuals associated with George Gustav Heye, Frederick W. Hodge, and Indians of North America. Correspondence to George Gustav Heye and Frederick W. Hodge, on various subjects. Some letters are not addressed to Heye or Hodge, but they likely collected them. Letters appear to have been collected and saved for the signatures of the writers. Signatures include many late nineteenth century and early twentieth century anthropologists, archaeologists, ethnologists, and individuals otherwise connected to Indians of North America. Some of the signatures are on cards; most are in correspondence that relates to the institutes, including the Bureau of American Ethnology, the Smithsonian, the Museum of the American Indian, Carnegie Museum, United States National Museum, Public Museum of the City of Milwaukee, Archaeological Institute of America, and their collections. Contents also include a song, notes about the Kiowa and Tanoan Indians, information about articles for American Anthropologist, requests for information from various books about Indians, and a National Certificate from the Cherokee Nation. In some cases there are multiple letters from one person.
Signatures include Cassius McDonald Barnes, Territorial Governor of Oklahoma; Samuel Alfred Barrett; Daniel C. Beard, two letters; A. H. Blackiston; Captain John Gregory Bourke; Charles Pickering Bowditch; Daniel Garrison Brinton; David Ives Bushnell, three letters; Lucien Carr; Alexander F. Chamberlain; Mabel Choate; Stuart Culin, four letters; J. T. Cunningham Cherkoee Nation; Edward S. Curtis, three letters; James Wheeler Davidson, see Schley letter; Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh; Edward Willard Deming; Frances Densmore, four letters; Fanny Dixon; Roland B. Dixon, two letters; George. T. Emmons, two letters; Jesse Walter Fewkes, two letters; Alice C. Fletcher; Barbara Freire-Marreco; Albert Samuel Gatschet; Pliny Earle Goddard; George Byron Gordon; George Bird Grinnell, three letters; John Peabody Harrington, four letters; Carl Vilhelm Hartman; H. O. S. Heistand, War Department, Assistant Adjutant General; Edgar Lee Hewett, two letters; Thor Heyerdahl, two letters; Walter James Hoffman; William Henry Holmes; Gaillard Hunt, see Twitchell letter; Ernest Ingersoll; Waldemar Jochelson; Charles Joseph Kappler; Herman Frederik Carel ten Kate; Minor C. Keith, two letters; Alfred Vincent Kidder; Alfred Louis Kroeber; Berthold Laufer; Nicolás León; Francis Ellington Leupp; Albert Buell Lewis; Ralph Linton; Samuel Kirkland Lothrop.
Other signatures include, Otis Tufton Mason, three letters; Washington Matthews; William John McGee; Joseph Deakins McGuire, in his daughter's hand; William Corless Mills; James Mooney, three letters; Clarence Bloomfield Moore, five letters; Warren King Moorehead; Adrien Gabriel Morice; Erland Nordenskiöld; Zelia Nutthall; Undecipherable signature from the Office of William Mathewson, A.K.A. Buffalo Bill, looks like J. T. Ogtrom, two letters; Charles Peabody, two letters; George Hubbard Pepper; Frederic Ward Putnam; Paul Rivet; Nelson A. Rockefeller; Walter Edmund Roth, three letters; Edward Sapir; Winfield Scott Schley, six letters; Harlan I. Smith, three letters; Edward Anthony Spitzka; Frederick Starr; John Reed Swanton; Cyrus Thomas; William Wallace Tooker; Alfred Marston Tozzer; James Hammond Trumbull; Ralph Emerson Twitchell, four letters; H. Newell Wardle; Lee F. Warner, see Kappler letter; and George Parker Winship.
RELATED MATERIALS
For additional letters from individuals in this collection see collection number 9065, Correspondence received by Frederick Webb Hodge.
{enter information about related materials here.}
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
Original Huntington Free Library box numbers have been retained. Box contains multiple unrelated collections. Folder numbers pertain to this specific collection only and are not folder order for the entire box.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondents A-M Folder 1
Series II. Correspondents N-Z Folder 2
Cite As:
George G. Heye autograph collection, #9063. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Autographs (manuscripts). Tanoan Indians. Kiowa Apache Indians. Chinook Indians. Indianists. Indians of North America. Heye, George G. (George Gustav), 1874-1957. Hodge, Frederick Webb, 1864-1956. Barnes, Cassius McDonald. Barrett, S. A. (Samuel Alfred), 1879-1965. Beard, Daniel Carter, 1850-1941. Blackiston, A. H. Boas, Franz, 1858-1942. Bourke, John Gregory, 1846-1896. Bowditch, Charles P. (Charles Pickering), 1842-1921. Brinton, Daniel G. (Daniel Garrison), 1837-1899. Bushnell, David I., Jr. (David Ives), 1875-1941. Carr, Lucien, 1829-1915. Chamberlain, Alexander Francis, 1865-1914. Choate, Mabel, 1870-1958. Culin, Stewart, 1858-1929. Cunningham, J. T. Curtis, Edward S., 1868-1952. Davidson, James Wheeler, 1872-1933. Dellenbaugh, Frederick Samuel, 1853-1935. Deming, Edwin Willard, 1860-1942. Densmore, Frances, 1867-1957. Dixon, Fanny. Dixon, Roland Burrage, 1875-1934. Emmons, George Thornton. Fewkes, Jesse Walter, 1850-1930. Fletcher, Alice C. (Alice Cunningham), 1838-1923. Freire-Marreco, Barbara W. (Barbara Whitchurch), 1879-1967. Gatschet, Albert S. (Albert Samuel), 1832-1907. Goddard, Pliny Earle, 1869-1928. Gordon, G. B. (George Byron), 1870-1927. Grinnell, George Bird, 1849-1938. Harrington, John Peabody. Hartman, Carl Vilhelm, 1862-1941. Heistand, H. O. S. Hewett, Edgar L. (Edgar Lee), 1865-1946. Heyerdahl, Thor. Hoffman, Walter James, 1846-1899. Holmes, William Henry, 1846-1933. Hunt, Gaillard, 1862-1924. Ingersoll, Ernest, 1852-1946. Jochelson, Waldemar, 1855-1937. Kappler, Charles Joseph, 1868-1946. Kate, Herman F. C. ten (Herman Frederik Carel), 1858-1931. Keith, Minor C. (Minor Cooper), 1848-1929. Kidder, Alfred Vincent, 1885-1963. Kroeber, A. L. (Alfred Louis), 1876-1960. Laufer, Berthold, 1874-1934. León, Nicolás, 1859-1929. Leupp, Francis E. (Francis Ellington), 1849-1918. Lewis, A. B. (Albert Buell), 1867-1940. Linton, Ralph, 1893-1953. Lothrop, S. K. (Samuel Kirkland), 1892-1965. Mason, Otis T., 1838-1908. Mathewson, William. Matthews, Washington, 1843-1905 McGee, W. J., 1853-1912. McGuire, Joseph Deakins. Mills, William C. (William Corless), 1860-1928. Mooney, James, 1861-1921. Moore, Clarence B. (Clarence Bloomfield), 1852-1936. Moorehead, Warren King, 1866-1939. Morice, A. G. (Adrien Gabriel), 1859-1938. Nordenskiöld, Erland, 1877-1932. Nuttall, Zelia, 1858-1933. Peabody, Charles, 1867-1939. Pepper, George H. (George Hubbard), 1873-1924. Putnam, F. W. (Frederic Ward), 1839-1915. Rivet, Paul, 1876-1958. Rockefeller, Nelson A. (Nelson Aldrich), 1908-1979. Roth, Walter E. (Walter Edmund), 1861?-1933. Sapir, Edward, 1884-1939. Schley, Winfield Scott, 1839-1911. Smith, Harlan Ingersoll, 1872-1940. Spitzka, Edward Anthony. Starr, Frederick, 1858-1933. Swanton, John Reed, 1873-1958. Thomas, Cyrus, 1825-1910. Tooker, William Wallace, 1848-1917. Tozzer, Alfred M. (Alfred Marston), 1877-1954. Trumbull, J. Hammond (James Hammond), 1821-1897. Twitchell, Ralph Emerson, 1859-1925. Wardle, H. Newell. Warner, Lee F. Willoughby, Charles Clark, 1857-1943. Winship, George Parker, 1871-1952. Archaeological Institute of America Carnegie Museum of Natural History Cherokee Nation Milwaukee Public Museum Museum of the American Indian, Heye Foundation Peabody Museum of Archaeology and Ethnology Smithsonian Institution Smithsonian Institution. Bureau of American Ethnology United States National Museum
